title: Migrations framework for on-disk format versioning
description:
Claude Code has a top-level migrations/ directory that upgrades on-disk formats (settings.json schema bumps, transcript reshaping, memory DB migrations). OpenClaudia has none — any future schema change to src/transcript.rs, chat_sessions/*.json, or memory.rs will silently corrupt existing data on upgrade.

## Scope
- New src/migrations/ module with a version registry
- Detect current version on startup from each managed store (settings, transcripts, memory)
- Run forward migrations in order (M1 -> M2 -> M3) with per-store granularity
- Keep a version sentinel file (.openclaudia/schema-versions.json)
- Integration points: main.rs startup, config::load_config, transcript module, memory.rs

## Why now
New transcript.rs JSONL format (b117e0a) has no migration path. First time we change the SerializedMessage envelope, users lose their history. Same for any future change to ChatSession JSON.
